fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Arizona Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Arizona - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity arizona, how to transfer occupational therapist license to arizona, occupational therapist compact arizona, arizona occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Arizona Board of Occupational Therapy Examiners
board.parent_dept: Arizona does not mandate state dietitian licensure. The national CDR credential is the recognized standard.
board.mailing_address: Arizona State Board of Occupational Therapy Examiners, 1740 W. Adams, Suite 3405, Phoenix, AZ 85007

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Arizona.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create Account and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Register for an account on the Arizona State Board of Occupational Therapy Examiners Online Portal (az.mylicense.com/bop) and complete the initial licensure application, including payment of the application fee.

steps.title: Obtain Arizona Fingerprint Clearance Card
steps.description: Apply for and receive an Arizona Fingerprint Clearance Card through the Arizona Department of Public Safety (DPS). A valid card is required for licensure.

steps.title: Pass the NBCOT Exam
steps.description: Successfully pass the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination. You must request NBCOT to send your official scores directly to the Arizona State Board of Occupational Therapy Examiners.

steps.title: Pass the Arizona Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Complete and pass the Arizona Jurisprudence Exam. This exam is typically administered online as part of the application process within the Arizona State Board of Occupational Therapy Examiners Online Portal.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official academic transcripts, verifying completion of an accredited occupational therapy program and supervised fieldwork, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Arizona State Board of Occupational Therapy Examiners.

steps.title: Await Application Review and Pay Initial License Fee
steps.description: Allow approximately 4 to 6 weeks for the Arizona State Board of Occupational Therapy Examiners to review your complete application. Upon approval, you will be notified to pay the initial license fee.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Arizona offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the Arizona Board of Occupational Therapy Examiners for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Arizona is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Arizona without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Arizona also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

faqs.question: How much does a Arizona OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $235. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Arizona have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Arizona has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Arizona?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Arizona while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Arizona is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Arizona without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Arizona also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.
